ddls:
CREATE TABLE "missions" (
  "mission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mission */,
  "mission_name" TEXT /* Name of the mission */,
  "start_timestamp" TEXT /* Start timestamp of the mission */,
  "end_timestamp" TEXT /* End timestamp of the mission (if applicable) */,
  "mission_status" TEXT /* Current status of the mission */,
  "mission_type" TEXT /* Type of the mission (e.g., earthquake, flood, fire) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the mission */,
  "commander_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mission commander */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Allocated budget for the mission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the mission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_data" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response data point */,
  "mission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the mission the data belongs to */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the response occurred */,
  "responder_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the responder or team responsible */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the resources used */,
  "inaction_stand_s" INTEGER /* Inaction standstill time */,
  "triage_count_green" INTEGER /* Triage count (green) */,
  "triage_count_yellow" INTEGER /* Triage count (yellow) */,
  "triage_count_red" INTEGER /* Triage count (red) */,
  "action_triage_s" INTEGER /* Action triage time */,
  "action_speedup_s" INTEGER /* Action speedup time */,
  "action_dig_rubble_s" INTEGER /* Action dig rubble time */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was recorded */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the response */,
  "casualty_count" INTEGER /* Number of casualties */,
  "injuries_count" INTEGER /* Number of injured individuals */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_data_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_data_mission_id FOREIGN KEY ("mission_id") REFERENCES missions ("mission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource allocation */,
  "mission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the mission the resource is allocated to */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource (e.g., personnel, equipment, supplies) */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the resource allocated */,
  "allocation_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the resource was allocated */,
  "resource_status" TEXT /* Current status of the resource */,
  "resource_model" TEXT /* Model of the resource */,
  "resource_cost" TEXT /* Cost of the resource */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Maintenance schedule of the resource */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resources_mission_id FOREIGN KEY ("mission_id") REFERENCES missions ("mission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "mission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the mission the location belongs to */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "coordinates" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_mission_id FOREIGN KEY ("mission_id") REFERENCES missions ("mission_id")
)
column_names: End timestamp of the mission (if applicable)
Type of the mission (e.g., earthquake, flood, fire)
Unique identifier for each response data point
Reference to the mission the data belongs to
Reference to the location where the response occurred
Reference to the responder or team responsible
